task: I need to send a short update to a few people about a work issue that’s taking longer than expected, and I want the message to be easy to follow without adding extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft it so it sounds professional and fits a normal work update?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a clean, normal work-update version:

> Hi everyone, I wanted to share a quick update on this issue. We’ve confirmed [what’s already confirmed], and we’re still verifying [what’s still open]. That’s the part taking a little longer, so I don’t want to give you a partial answer. I’ll send the next update by [day/time].

If you want it a little warmer:

> Hi everyone, I know this has taken longer than expected. We’ve confirmed [what’s confirmed], and we’re still checking [what’s open]. I want to make sure we have the right answer before I close the loop, and I’ll update you by [day/time].

If you want, I can also make it:
- **more direct**
- **more casual**
- or **manager-style**

user_simulator.text: Pretty close, but I need it a little more specific and less template-y. I don’t want the bracketed placeholders or the “I know this has taken longer” version sounding too apologetic. Just a straightforward update that says it’s still in progress, what’s confirmed, what’s still being checked, and when they’ll hear back. Keep it professional, normal, and tight. If you can, give me one version that sounds like I’m sending it to coworkers and one that sounds a bit more manager-level.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are two tighter versions.

### Coworker version
> Hi everyone — quick update: we’ve confirmed the core issue, and we’re still checking the remaining details before it’s finalized. I don’t want to give a partial answer and have to change it later. I’ll send the next update by [day/time].

### Manager-level version
> Hi everyone — the issue is still in progress. We’ve confirmed the main facts, and we’re still verifying the remaining details before we can close it out. I’ll provide the next update by [day/time].

If you want, I can make either one:
- a little warmer
- a little firmer
- or even shorter for Slack/text

user_simulator.text:
Closer. The manager one is fine, but the coworker version still feels a little generic. I want it to actually say what’s confirmed and what’s still being checked, not just “core issue” and “remaining details.” Same for the manager one — tighter, but still a little vague.

Give me versions that sound like a real work update, not a template. Short, plain, and specific enough that people know what’s done, what isn’t, and when they’ll hear back. If you need to, use placeholders, but make them obvious and practical — not the bracket-heavy style.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are tighter, more natural versions with practical placeholders.

### Coworker version
> Hi everyone — we’ve confirmed **the pay records for last week are incomplete**, and we’re still checking **the missing hours and the approval trail** before anything is finalized. I’ll send the next update by **[day/time]**.

### Manager-level version
> Hi everyone — we’ve confirmed **the records don’t match yet**, and we’re still verifying **the hours, approvals, and final calculation** before this is closed out. I’ll provide the next update by **[day/time]**.

### If you want it even more plain
> Hi everyone — we’ve confirmed **there’s still a mismatch in the records**, and we’re checking **the hours and approvals** before we finalize anything. Next update by **[day/time]**.

If you want, I can also make these:
- **payroll-specific**
- **more neutral**
- or **short enough for Slack**

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this works. The coworker and manager versions both sound like an actual work update, not a canned template. Short, plain, specific enough. Good.
